title: Waffle Iron Day: Grid-Lock Goodness!
story: Every June 29, waffle lovers fire up their waffle irons to celebrate National Waffle Iron Day! People dust off their trusty kitchen gadgets and get creative with batters, making everything from classic breakfast waffles to savory potato waffles and even waffle brownies. Social media fills with photos of perfectly golden grids and wild waffle experiments. Some families host waffle parties where everyone brings different toppings like fresh berries, whipped cream, or maple syrup. The day celebrates the amazing invention that turns simple batter into crispy, syrup-catching masterpieces with those perfect little squares!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Waffle Iron Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative waffle recipe you can imagine making?

title: Crunch Time for Almond Buttercrunch Lovers!
story: Every June 29, candy lovers celebrate National Almond Buttercrunch Day by indulging in this sweet, crunchy treat! Almond buttercrunch is made by coating buttery toffee with chocolate and rolling it in chopped almonds. People mark this special day by buying boxes from candy shops, making homemade batches in their kitchens, or sharing pieces with friends and family. Some bakeries offer special discounts on almond buttercrunch treats, while social media fills up with photos of people enjoying their favorite crunchy confections.
activity_question: What are the main ingredients that make almond buttercrunch so special?
activity_choices: Buttery toffee, chocolate, and chopped almonds
Marshmallows, graham crackers, and chocolate chips
activity_answer: Buttery toffee, chocolate, and chopped almonds
bonus_fact: Some candy makers use up to three different types of chocolate in one buttercrunch recipe!

title: Say Cheese on Camera Day!
story: Every June 29, photographers everywhere celebrate National Camera Day by snapping extra special pictures! People dust off their old cameras, try new photography tricks, and share their favorite shots on social media. Some families organize photo walks around their neighborhoods, while others dig through old photo albums to remember amazing memories. Camera stores often host fun contests and workshops to teach kids cool techniques like taking close-up pictures of bugs or capturing the perfect sunset. It's the perfect day to become a photography detective and discover the world through your lens!
activity_question: What do some families do to celebrate National Camera Day?
activity_choices: Organize photo walks around their neighborhoods
Hide their cameras in secret places
Only take pictures of their pets
activity_answer: Organize photo walks around their neighborhoods
follow_up_question: What would be the most interesting thing in your neighborhood to photograph?
